zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Joan Scotts於1495年出生在英國薩默塞特可福可納時，她的父親Sir William Scott36歲，母親Sybil Lewknor43歲。她於1513年與Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1587年10月20日在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇 阿爾蓋特享年92歲，埋葬在英國戴文希維可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Joan Scotts于1495年出生在英国薩默塞特可福可納时，她的父亲Sir William Scott36岁，母亲Sybil Lewknor43岁。她于1513年与Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1587年10月20日在英国英格兰倫敦聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇 阿爾蓋特享年92岁，埋葬在英国戴文希維可。

nameMeanings.name: Scotts
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: variant of Scott with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Scott
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (Down): habitational and ethnic name from Middle English Scot ‘man from Scotland’. There is no evidence that the surname denoted either of the earlier senses of Scot as ‘(Gaelic-speaking) Irishman’ or ‘man from Alba’, the Gaelic-speaking region of Scotland north of the river Forth. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English and Scottish: from the rare Middle English personal name Scot (Old English Scott, possibly also Old Norse Skotr), only certainly attested in northern England.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Of uncertain origin. If not simply an invention, it may have been devised as a feminine equivalent of   Alan   (of which it is an anagram), or a shortened form of   Alana .  It seems to have been first used by the American film actress  Lana Turner  (  1920–1995 ), whose original name was  Julia .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Joan Scotts was born in 1495, in Crewkerne, Somerset, England, her father, Sir William Scott, was 36 and her mother, Sybil Lewknor, was 43. She married Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court in 1513. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 20 October 1587, in Saint Botolph's Church Aldgate, London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 92, and was buried in Highweek, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Joan Scotts est née en 1495, à Crewkerne, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Scott, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Sybil Lewknor, avait 43 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court en 1513. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 20 octobre 1587, à Saint Botolph's Church Aldgate, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 92 ans, et a été enterrée à Highweek, Devon, Angleterre.

Lei sposato Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court nel 1513. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. ko.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Scotts(이)가 1495년 영국 서머세트 크레w커니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Scott(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sybil Lewknor(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 1513년 Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1587년 10월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 세인트 벗프'스 c후르치 알드가트에서 92세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 히그h위크에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court em 1513. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 20 de outubro de 1587, em Saint Botolph's Church Aldgate, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 92 anos, e foi sepultada em Highweek, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.St Botolph Aldgate, London, England: сеинт ботолф'с чурч алджэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Joan Scotts родилась в 1495 г. в местности крюкирн, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William Scott было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Sybil Lewknor, 43. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court в 1513 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 20 октября 1587 г. в местности сеинт ботолф'с чурч алджэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 92 лет и была похоронена в местности хаивик, девон, Англия.
